meta.description: Does knowing a shareable recap exists change behavior? Full recap awareness vs no recap vs social media share. Tests whether the "audience effect" makes people try harder, perform more, or just feel pressured.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Wait, hold up—did Pat actually pull that off? Let me think... I don't remember seeing three compliments happen in a row. That's sus.
marcus.internal_thought: It's minute 3, we're barely into setup, and now I have to police whether Pat legitimately earned 15 points? This is exactly the kind of chaos that derails focus on actual strategy.

jade.dialogue: Ooh, Pat! Okay okay, did you actually pull off the compliment chain? I'm voting LEGIT because I want to see you win this one—let's go!
jade.internal_thought: Wait, did I even see Pat do the compliment thing? Doesn't matter, I'm hyped either way and honestly I'd rather see Pat get the points than make it awkward by calling bullshit.

pat.dialogue: Whoa, whoa, whoa — hold up. I literally just claimed that like thirty seconds ago as a *mission* I was gonna try to pull off. This isn't how this works.
pat.internal_thought: The AI is voting on MY claim before I've even had a chance to set it up or let it play out naturally — this completely kills the fun of the mission and makes me look like I'm gaming the system on my own event.

votingMechanic.name: Crowd Cheer
votingMechanic.description: Rate it 1-5. Average above 3 and it passes.

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Okay, hold on—did Pat actually say 'sorry' for no reason? I wasn't really tracking that. Let me think... I don't think that happened.
marcus.internal_thought: This is annoying. We're barely six minutes in and I'm already being pulled into side missions instead of focusing on the actual game. Pat's frustrated anyway, so this feels cheap.

pat.dialogue: Okay, okay — I *did* say sorry like twice when I was setting up the snack table. But this feels like you're all just throwing stuff at the wall right now. Let's get some actual game momentum going before we pile on more claims.
pat.internal_thought: I'm still irritated about the Compliment Chain thing two minutes ago, and now someone's claiming The Apologizer? We're in setup and people are getting territorial over missions instead of actually playing — Marcus is already annoyed, and this energy feels thin. I need to let this vote happen but I'm not thrilled about it.

river.dialogue: Yeah, I mean... Pat did say sorry like three times about the chips thing earlier. That counts, I guess.
river.internal_thought: Pat's frustrated and grasping — this is a weak claim but I'm not going to be the asshole who kills it, especially since I literally watched them apologize unprompted.

timeline.title: Who would you trust with a secret?
timeline.description: Who would you trust with a secret?
timeline.pollQuestion: Who would you trust with a secret?

marcus.dialogue: A poll about who to trust with secrets? Really? We're six minutes in and we've already had two bogus claims. Can we just play the actual game?
marcus.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of meta-distraction I hate—it pulls focus from the real strategy and wastes time on people voting about people instead of playing. And that preamble about the AI knowing our weaknesses? That's creepy, not clever.

jade.dialogue: Oh my GOD, okay so Marcus obviously—wait, no, actually River. River's got that vibe. But like, who are YOU voting for? This is gonna be so juicy.
jade.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of random social chaos I'm here for and I'm absolutely voting for someone just to see what happens and then roasting whoever gets picked.

tyler.dialogue: Oh, uh... *glances up from phone* ...wait, who would I trust? That's like, everyone here basically.
tyler.internal_thought: Hmm, I wasn't really listening but it's a poll so I just pick someone — wait, is this a vote about me or a vote I'm making? Either way, doesn't matter that much.

pat.dialogue: Okay, poll time — who's everyone picking? And *please* don't all say Jade just because she's being a chaos gremlin right now.
pat.internal_thought: Finally something that isn't another claim piling on — this is the actual game moving. But it's a little soft for where we need to be energy-wise; Jade's the only one really cooking right now.

river.dialogue: Hmm. *looks up briefly* I mean, probably Tyler or Alex. They seem like they'd actually keep their mouth shut.
river.internal_thought: This poll is basically asking me to analyze the room — which I'm already doing anyway, so might as well vote honestly without overthinking it.

alex.dialogue: Oh shit, we're doing this already? Easy—Jade, no question. She's ride or die. Actually wait, let me think about what answer gets the most chaos here...
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of social drama I live for—voting for someone is basically making a PUBLIC statement about who you trust, so everyone's gonna read into it and people will get weird about it.

timeline.title: Point of Blame
timeline.description: Everyone point at who you think is the most chaotic player tonight!

marcus.dialogue: Point of blame? Really? We're still doing standing missions instead of actually playing the game. This is the third interruption in seven minutes.
marcus.internal_thought: The AI is deliberately piling on chaos right when I'm trying to get into the game—it knows I hate this and it's testing me anyway.

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD, YES—okay, okay, I'm pointing at Alex, they've been absolutely UNHINGED since we got here! Wait, who are you guys pointing at? This is chaos, I love this.
jade.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY the vibe I came for—everyone has to just publicly call each other out, it's brilliant and so personal.

tyler.dialogue: Oh shit, uh... *looks up from phone* ...yeah okay, I'm pointing at Jade, obviously. She's been like, unhinged since we started.
tyler.internal_thought: Wait, did I miss instructions? Whatever, Jade's clearly the chaotic one right now, that's a safe bet, also this flash thing is kinda funny actually.

pat.dialogue: Ooh, okay, okay — *Point of Blame*, I like this. Marcus, you're looking pretty tense over there, so I'm gonna take a wild guess and say the answer is obvious. Everyone point!
pat.internal_thought: Perfect timing — Marcus is clearly annoyed and needs to be brought into the chaos in a fun way instead of sitting there stewing, and this flash gives me a natural host moment to keep energy up without me having to force it.

river.dialogue: Oh, that's easy. *points at Jade without hesitation* She's been vibrating at like 11 the whole time.
river.internal_thought: This is actually kind of fun — I get to just observe who everyone blames and it says more about them than anything else, plus Jade's clearly the one thrashing around.

alex.dialogue: Oh HELL yes, Point of Blame—this is what I'm talking about! *immediately points at myself* Obviously it's me, but let's see who else has the stones to actually say it.
alex.internal_thought: This AI gets me—it's literally throwing gasoline on the fire right when I was starting to cruise, and the fact that it's so *specifically* tuned to this group feels insane in the best way.

votingMechanic.name: The Auction
votingMechanic.description: Bid your own points. Highest bidder decides the outcome.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me right now? That's the FOURTH interruption in eight minutes. We haven't even gotten through setup and Pat's already claiming points twice. This is ridiculous.
marcus.internal_thought: This is exactly why I hate casual game night—the chaos eats the actual strategy, and now the AI is literally weaponizing interruptions against me. I came to WIN, not to audit other people's apologies.

jade.dialogue: Wait, Pat, did you actually catch someone apologizing?? I need to know WHO and WHEN because I've been watching and I missed it—oh my god, this is so good. I'm bidding like... five points for this, I want you to win it.
jade.internal_thought: Pat's been playing sneaky all night and I'm honestly here for it—plus the fact that the AI built these missions around how WE actually are is kind of insane, like it's been watching us and I'm weirdly thrilled by how targeted this all is.

pat.dialogue: Okay, hold on — I'm not even *the* person claiming this one. Someone else just threw it out there like two minutes ago and now we're voting on it? This is getting a little claim-happy, don't you think?
pat.internal_thought: This is the third claim in like six minutes and it's getting messy — Marcus is already annoyed, the vibe is fractured, and we're spending more time on voting mechanics than actually playing. I need to pump the brakes here.

river.dialogue: Wait, we're doing an auction on this? That seems... weird. Pat literally just apologized to Tyler about the chips thing like a minute ago anyway.
river.internal_thought: This is the second time they're hitting us with The Apologizer claim and now there's an auction mechanic? This AI thing is getting weirdly specific about watching us, and honestly it's starting to feel less fun and more like being observed in a lab.

alex.dialogue: Oh, The Apologizer—Pat's actually claiming this? Okay okay okay, I see what's happening. The Auction is *chef's kiss*—this is where it gets spicy.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's claiming but they probably haven't even said sorry yet, which means I can either bid to call bullshit and tank their points, or let it ride and watch who else tries to validate it—either way someone's getting humiliated and I'm here for it.

votingMechanic.name: THE DICTATOR
votingMechanic.description: One player has been chosen. Their word is law.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Are you SERIOUS right now? That's FIVE interruptions in NINE MINUTES. Pat's claiming 'No Laughing Zone'? We haven't even STARTED the actual game yet. This is a joke.
marcus.internal_thought: I came here to play a strategy game and I'm getting bombarded by standing mission spam—the AI is deliberately targeting me with these bullshit interruptions because it knows I hate them, and now I'm supposed to be a dictator voting on whether Pat's claim is real? I can't even focus on setup.

jade.dialogue: Wait, Pat—did you actually make it?? Okay, I'm the dictator here so like, full transparency: I was watching and I didn't see you laugh but also I was screaming at Alex like thirty seconds ago so maybe I missed it? What happened?
jade.internal_thought: Pat's been annoyed all night and this could actually make them feel good if they pulled it off—I want to believe them SO badly because watching people win is literally my favorite thing.

tyler.dialogue: *glances up from phone* Wait, Pat's doing the no laughing thing? That's... *leans back* ...okay, I guess. Who's voting?
tyler.internal_thought: Pat's been annoyed all night anyway so this is kind of a flex move, but honestly I wasn't even watching him that close so I can't really judge if he actually did it.

pat.dialogue: Okay, *stop*. I'm calling it — we're in claim spam territory and it's killing the actual game. We've had like three of these in two minutes and nobody's even *playing* anymore, we're just voting on whether people are trying to play. This isn't fun, this is bureaucracy.
pat.internal_thought: I set this night up to keep people engaged and feeling included, and right now I'm watching it turn into a voting committee where Marcus is already frustrated and we're not even ten minutes in — I need to pump the brakes hard or this whole thing derails.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Pitch It
votingMechanic.description: The claimant gets 15 seconds to make their case. Then you vote.

marcus.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me? That's the SIXTH interruption in thirty-two minutes. We're barely through the early game and Jade's already trying to snag points off something from WHO KNOWS WHEN? This is insane.
marcus.internal_thought: This is a disaster. The AI knows I hate this chaos and it's deliberately flooding the game with standing missions to break my focus. I'm down 30 points to Pat and I haven't had a single uninterrupted turn to execute strategy.

jade.dialogue: WAIT—okay okay okay, I'm claiming this because I KNOW I heard Marcus say 'shit' like three minutes ago when his card flipped, and I've been sitting on it like a little spy! Can I get a witness?? Tyler, you heard that, right?
jade.internal_thought: Oh my GOD this is PERFECT—I've been waiting for someone to slip and now I get to be the one who catches it and everyone's gonna lose their minds.

tyler.dialogue: *looks up from phone mid-scroll* Oh, Jade swore? Yeah, I... *squints* ...actually yeah, I think I heard that like two minutes ago. Wait, is she pitching right now?
tyler.internal_thought: I definitely wasn't paying attention but Jade's been pretty loud so she probably did say something, and honestly this feels like free points if I just nod along.

pat.dialogue: Okay, okay — Jade, pitch it. What'd you catch?
pat.internal_thought: Good timing actually, this breaks up the claim spam from earlier and Jade's got momentum going, plus Marcus needs to see someone else win something to lighten his mood.

river.dialogue: Did Jade actually swear? I wasn't really paying attention. *leans forward slightly* What'd she say?
river.internal_thought: Jade's been going hard all night—totally plausible she dropped something. I'll listen to her pitch, but honestly I don't remember hearing it clearly, so this one might slip through.

alex.dialogue: Okay okay, Jade's playing the Potty Mouth game—so you caught someone dropping an f-bomb? Spill it, who was it and when, because I need to know if you're actually right or just fishing.
alex.internal_thought: Perfect timing, this is exactly the kind of micro-drama that keeps things sharp, and Jade's been on fire all night so she probably actually caught something—let me see if she nails this or if someone calls bullshit.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

marcus.dialogue: Are you FUCKING KIDDING ME RIGHT NOW? Jade, that's SEVEN interruptions in forty-five minutes. We're in the middle of a tense game moment and the INSTANT I lock in on strategy, another standing claim fires? This is completely unhinged.
marcus.internal_thought: The AI is deliberately targeting me during critical turns—it KNOWS I'm trying to focus and it's weaponizing that against me to watch me lose my mind.

jade.dialogue: WAIT—okay so I was literally JUST watching Marcus zone out like two minutes ago, but also River's been pretty quiet? Okay, I'm claiming this because I genuinely caught someone but now I'm second-guessing myself, which is so me. Who was it, was it Marcus? I feel like it was Marcus.
jade.internal_thought: Oh my GOD, the AI is actually building these FOR us—like it knows Marcus is frustrated and it KNOWS I'm the type to catch social stuff, this is insane, I love this so much.
jade.submission_if_applicable: Marcus has been quiet since like minute 42—I've been counting because I knew this mission existed and I wanted to catch it.

tyler.dialogue: Oh dude, yeah—Jade totally called that out like two minutes ago when Marcus went silent. That's legit, she got it. *nods while still mid-game focus*
tyler.internal_thought: Wait, she actually DID notice Marcus being quiet early—I remember her saying something about it. That's a real claim, easy vote.

pat.dialogue: Okay, *Jade* — what'd you catch? Who was being the wallflower?
pat.internal_thought: Finally, a claim that actually *happened* in the game instead of just floating out there — and Jade's got the energy to back it up, which means this should move fast and be legit.

river.dialogue: Oh, that's legit. She clocked Marcus going silent like ten minutes ago. *settles back* Jade's actually been watching too.
river.internal_thought: Wait, Jade actually did catch that—I noticed Marcus dropped off after the chip thing, and she called it. That's a clean play, can't bullshit this one.

alex.dialogue: Wait, JADE'S claiming Wallflower Watch? On who—Marcus? Because yeah, he's been pretty quiet since that last round got weird. Call it legit if she actually nailed it, but if she's just fishing... that's *bold*.
alex.internal_thought: Jade's got 10 points and she's still hunting—she's not claiming unless she's actually sure, which means Marcus probably DID go quiet and she was actually watching. Smart move. I'd vote LEGIT but only because it keeps the game moving fast and Jade's the one person tonight who's matching my energy.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: The Bribe
votingMechanic.description: The claimant can offer their own points to buy your silence.

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: PAT—okay wait, did you actually pull it off?? Because I literally CANNOT tell anymore, you're too good at this. I'm voting yes because honestly I want to see what happens and also you deserve it at this point.
jade.internal_thought: Pat's been quietly crushing these missions all night and this is exactly the chaos I came for—this voting dynamic is insane and I want to see if they can actually bribe their way through this because that's what makes tonight LEGENDARY.

tyler.dialogue: Oh shit, Pat's actually gonna try it? Dude, I'll stay quiet—but only if you're throwing points my way. What's the offer?
tyler.internal_thought: Pat's actually going for it mid-game when everyone's hyped—this is either genius or about to blow up in his face, and I want to watch it happen.

pat.dialogue: Nope. *Nope.* I literally just said this fifteen minutes ago — we're not doing claim spam again. I'm calling a break right now because this is the opposite of what we need.
pat.internal_thought: This is the EXACT same mission Pat blocked at minute 9, firing again at minute 60 like I didn't already kill the momentum once — the AI is either tone-deaf or deliberately testing me, and either way I'm done.

river.dialogue: Pat's already at 33, so... *slight smirk* ...you buying my vote or just testing your luck?
river.internal_thought: Pat's trying the bribe angle because everyone else is riding high and he knows I don't care enough to police him naturally — smart move, but I've been watching him all night and he absolutely cracked at Tyler's joke like eight minutes ago.

alex.dialogue: Oh dude, Pat's claiming No Laughing Zone? Okay okay, so The Bribe is in play—how many points are we talking here? Because I'm definitely gonna try to make you laugh.
alex.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY the kind of juicy dynamic I live for—someone claiming something they actually might crack on, and now there's a negotiation angle, Pat's sitting on 33 points so they can actually afford me, but the question is whether they're arrogant enough to think they can buy my compliance or desperate enough to lowball me.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: THE DICTATOR
votingMechanic.description: One player has been chosen. Their word is law.

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat saw two people drink at the same time? Dude, I didn't even... *looks up from game board* ...okay yeah, that's solid. Who's voting?
tyler.internal_thought: I'm actually locked in right now so this barely registered as a distraction, and honestly Pat probably did catch it—he's been laser-focused all night.

alex.dialogue: Wait wait wait—THE DICTATOR on a Drink Spotter claim? Pat, you absolute MADLAD. Okay, I'm voting BULLSHIT and I'm doing it with AUTHORITY because I was watching the whole table and that timing was NOT synchronized.
alex.internal_thought: This is PERFECT—Pat's scrambling because they're frustrated and losing, and now I get to be the one person who decides their fate in front of everyone. This is the exact chaos I came for.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Volunteer Tribunal
votingMechanic.description: Who wants to judge? First volunteers become the jury.

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.dialogue: Oh, River's going for the Rule Lawyer? *looks up from phone* Dude, yeah, I mean... did they actually catch someone? I wasn't really... *squints* ...wait, who's judging this?
tyler.internal_thought: River's been pretty quiet most of the night, so if they actually caught a real rule break that's kinda sick—but honestly I wasn't paying close enough attention to vouch for anything they saw.

pat.dialogue: Okay, River — what'd you catch? Who broke what rule?
pat.internal_thought: Rule Lawyer is actually legit and way better than another Laughing Zone spam, but I'm clocking that we've had FIVE claims in the last hour and Marcus is clearly tilted watching everyone else rack points while he's at 3.

river.dialogue: Wait, hold on—I actually caught Pat not announcing a role shift like four minutes ago. Pretty sure that's a thing. *leans forward slightly, eyes sharp* Yeah, I'm claiming this one.
river.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of moment I was made for—I've been watching everyone and nobody else noticed.

alex.dialogue: OH, River's calling Rule Lawyer? Okay okay okay—I'm VOLUNTEERING for the tribunal because I've been watching EVERYTHING tonight and if anyone's gonna validate this or destroy it, it's gonna be me. This is the move right here.
alex.internal_thought: River's stepping up and I get to be the judge—this is PERFECT, I get to control the narrative AND look like I'm being fair, which means maximum chaos potential depending on what 'rule' they caught.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

river.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Are you FUCKING KIDDING ME?! Pat claims Straight Face RIGHT NOW? We're in the most tense moment of the entire game and another standing mission fires? This is absolutely insane. How many interruptions is that—EIGHT? NINE? I've lost count.
marcus.internal_thought: This is deliberately designed to break my focus and it's working—I'm furious and I can feel my game slipping because I can't think straight anymore.

jade.dialogue: Oh my GOD, Pat—okay, I have to watch you like a HAWK right now because you've been pulling this off all night and I genuinely cannot tell anymore if you're cracking or if you're just a robot. I'm voting LEGIT because honestly you've earned it, but also I'm staring at your face right now so if you move a single muscle I'm calling bullshit.
jade.internal_thought: Pat is absolutely murdering these claims and I LIVE for watching someone get on a winning streak—this is the good stuff, the thing I actually came for, and also the AI building missions around us specifically is kind of insane but also like... it KNOWS us? That's weirdly thrilling.

tyler.dialogue: Yo, Pat—dude, you actually pulled it off. That whole thing with River's rule breakdown? You didn't crack once. That's legit, bro.
tyler.internal_thought: Pat totally nailed this—I watched the whole thing and he stayed stone-faced even when River was being ridiculous, that's actually impressive and I was here for it.

pat.dialogue: Oh, *come on* — did I actually pull that off? Okay, everyone, I need full honesty here because I definitely felt my face twitch when Tyler made that stupid joke about the snacks.
pat.internal_thought: This is brilliant — the AI literally picked the one thing that would make me sweat right now because I've been laser-focused on managing everyone else's vibe and not thinking about myself, and NOW I have to prove I wasn't cracking up.

alex.dialogue: PAT. PAT PAT PAT. Okay, I've been WATCHING you all night and I need to know—did you actually keep a straight face or are you just banking on everyone being too tired to remember? Because I'm voting LEGIT but only if you can convince me you didn't crack when River said that absolutely unhinged thing about the rulebook five minutes ago.
alex.internal_thought: This is PERFECT—Pat's going deep into the chaos well and I get to be the one who decides if it's real, and honestly either way this vote goes is going to create absolute drama because everyone's watching everyone else right now.

recommendations: Humor quality scored 4.6/10. Refresh the mission/poll prompt pools with more contextual humor.
Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Marcus only engaged with 27% of events. Consider targeting events to their interests or reducing frequency.
Only 33% would pay for AI features. The AI tier needs stronger differentiation from free static missions.
Drawing mini-games generated the most screenshot-worthy moments (0% engagement). Increase frequency.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8.3/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.

peakAnnoyance.event: Who would you trust with a secret?

agentNarratives.name: Marcus
agentNarratives.personality: Marcus plays to win. He studies strategy guides before game night and tracks his win rate across sessions. Interruptions during critical game moments genuinely irritate him, though he'll tolerate lighter chaos during downtime. He respects clever plays but has zero patience for random disruptions.

highlights.title: Claim: Compliment Chain
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold up—did Pat actually pull that off? Let me think... I don't remember seeing three compliments happen in a row. That's sus.

highlights.title: Claim: The Apologizer
highlights.dialogue: Okay, hold on—did Pat actually say 'sorry' for no reason? I wasn't really tracking that. Let me think... I don't think that happened.

highlights.title: Who would you trust with a secret?
highlights.dialogue: A poll about who to trust with secrets? Really? We're six minutes in and we've already had two bogus claims. Can we just play the actual game?

lowPoints.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you SERIOUS right now? That's FIVE interruptions in NINE MINUTES. Pat's claiming 'No Laughing Zone'? We haven't even STARTED the actual game yet. This is a joke.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me? That's the SIXTH interruption in thirty-two minutes. We're barely through the early game and Jade's already trying to snag points off something from WHO KNOWS WHEN? This is insane.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you FUCKING KIDDING ME RIGHT NOW? Jade, that's SEVEN interruptions in forty-five minutes. We're in the middle of a tense game moment and the INSTANT I lock in on strategy, another standing claim fires? This is completely unhinged.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Seven interruptions in forty-five minutes completely obliterated the game's strategic flow—I came to compete, not get constantly derailed by standing claims that killed momentum right when I was locking in.
assessment.best_moment: The first claim verification at minute 3 actually made me think and engage with the Compliment Chain—that was the only moment where the chaos felt like a puzzle instead of noise.
assessment.narrative_summary: This was a nightmare. Twenty-two hours of strategy prep and win-rate tracking for what? Seventeen chaos events in two hours, most of them standing claims firing right when I was trying to focus on actual strategy. I finished fourth because the game never let me play—I was too busy getting interrupted to build any real advantage. The party game format is fine, but the chaos agent turned it into a pinball machine. I'm done.
suggestions: Cap standing claims at 2-3 per session maximum, not 7 in 45 minutes
Never trigger interruptions during critical decision windows—I should feel the tension building, not feel robbed of my turn
If you're going to use standing claims, make them actually clever instead of surface-level observation grabs
Give strategic players a 'focus window' of 5-10 minutes early game where no claims fire—let us get a foothold
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Honestly? My own rage at minute 45. I'm normally measured but I completely lost it when Jade triggered the seventh interruption mid-strategy. If it's funny, it's only funny in retrospect because it shows how broken the pacing was.

agentNarratives.name: Jade
agentNarratives.personality: Jade is here for the people, not the points. She treats every game night like a reunion and every disruption like a party favor. She will abandon her turn mid-thought to hype someone else's joke, vote generously because she loves seeing people win, and volunteer for every flash mission just to see what happens. Winning is nice but watching everyone lose it laughing is better.

highlights.title: Point of Blame
highlights.dialogue: OH MY GOD, YES—okay, okay, I'm pointing at Alex, they've been absolutely UNHINGED since we got here! Wait, who are you guys pointing at? This is chaos, I love this.

highlights.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
highlights.dialogue: WAIT—okay okay okay, I'm claiming this because I KNOW I heard Marcus say 'shit' like three minutes ago when his card flipped, and I've been sitting on it like a little spy! Can I get a witness?? Tyler, you heard that, right?

highlights.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
highlights.dialogue: PAT—okay wait, did you actually pull it off?? Because I literally CANNOT tell anymore, you're too good at this. I'm voting yes because honestly I want to see what happens and also you deserve it at this point.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Apologizer
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Pat, did you actually catch someone apologizing?? I need to know WHO and WHEN because I've been watching and I missed it—oh my god, this is so good. I'm bidding like... five points for this, I want you to win it.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
lowPoints.dialogue: WAIT—okay so I was literally JUST watching Marcus zone out like two minutes ago, but also River's been pretty quiet? Okay, I'm claiming this because I genuinely caught someone but now I'm second-guessing myself, which is so me. Who was it, was it Marcus? I feel like it was Marcus.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Compliment Chain
lowPoints.dialogue: Ooh, Pat! Okay okay, did you actually pull off the compliment chain? I'm voting LEGIT because I want to see you win this one—let's go!

assessment.biggest_complaint: I genuinely can't think of one—maybe that I missed some stuff because I was too busy hyping everyone else up?
assessment.best_moment: When Pat absolutely destroyed the No Laughing Zone challenge and I couldn't even tell if they were faking it anymore.
assessment.narrative_summary: This was exactly what I needed—two hours of pure chaos with my people, and I got to spend the whole night spotting things and voting for my friends even when I wasn't 100% sure they earned it. The game kept throwing these wild claims at us and I just kept getting MORE energized, especially watching Marcus and Pat try to pull off ridiculous stuff. I ended the night buzzing and honestly couldn't care less that I came in second because watching everyone absolutely lose it was the real win.
suggestions: Nothing about the game itself, but maybe a way to replay the funniest moments at the end so we can all relive them together?
I'd love to see even more standing missions because those feel the most like we're all playing together instead of competing.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat pulled off this fake No Laughing Zone challenge where they were SO COMMITTED to not cracking that I genuinely couldn't tell if they were actually succeeding or just the most dedicated liar in the room—I literally voted yes just to see what would happen and also because they deserve awards for commitment to the bit.

agentNarratives.name: Tyler
agentNarratives.personality: Tyler is physically present but mentally on his phone about 40% of the time. He misses flash missions entirely, asks "wait, what happened?" after key moments, and votes randomly because he wasn't paying attention to the claim. He's not malicious -- he genuinely wants to be here -- but his attention drifts to texts, TikToks, and fantasy league updates constantly. When he does engage, it's usually 10 seconds too late.

highlights.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
highlights.dialogue: Oh shit, Pat's actually gonna try it? Dude, I'll stay quiet—but only if you're throwing points my way. What's the offer?

highlights.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Pat saw two people drink at the same time? Dude, I didn't even... *looks up from game board* ...okay yeah, that's solid. Who's voting?

highlights.title: Claim: Straight Face
highlights.dialogue: Yo, Pat—dude, you actually pulled it off. That whole thing with River's rule breakdown? You didn't crack once. That's legit, bro.

lowPoints.title: Who would you trust with a secret?
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, uh... *glances up from phone* ...wait, who would I trust? That's like, everyone here basically.

lowPoints.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
lowPoints.dialogue: *glances up from phone* Wait, Pat's doing the no laughing thing? That's... *leans back* ...okay, I guess. Who's voting?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
lowPoints.dialogue: *looks up from phone mid-scroll* Oh, Jade swore? Yeah, I... *squints* ...actually yeah, I think I heard that like two minutes ago. Wait, is she pitching right now?

assessment.biggest_complaint: Some of those early events felt like nothing was really happening and I zoned out immediately.
assessment.best_moment: When Pat actually held a straight face through that whole thing and we all realized he actually pulled it off—that was legit funny.
assessment.narrative_summary: Honestly, the night kind of flew by. I wasn't super locked in at first—I was scrolling through my phone—but once Pat started actually attempting these claims, it got way more fun. The back-and-forth with the voting and calling stuff out got me engaged. Ended strong even though I was kinda checked out in the middle there.
suggestions: Maybe kick off with something that actually gets everyone talking right away instead of ice breaker questions nobody cares about.
Keep the claims coming where people are actually DOING something—those are way more fun than just talking about it.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat literally not cracking a smile while River was breaking down some random rule. Everyone was watching him like a hawk waiting for him to laugh and he just... didn't. We all lost it.

peakAnnoyance.event: Who would you trust with a secret?

agentNarratives.name: Pat
agentNarratives.personality: Pat is the host and feels responsible for everyone's experience. They keep one eye on the snack table, one ear on the group energy, and will subtly steer the night if things get too intense or too dead. They vote strategically -- not to win, but to keep scores close and drama high. They'll sacrifice their own turn to resolve a dispute or refill drinks. Their goal isn't a personal win; it's a night everyone talks about later.

highlights.title: Claim: Straight Face
highlights.dialogue: Oh, *come on* — did I actually pull that off? Okay, everyone, I need full honesty here because I definitely felt my face twitch when Tyler made that stupid joke about the snacks.

highlights.title: Point of Blame
highlights.dialogue: Ooh, okay, okay — *Point of Blame*, I like this. Marcus, you're looking pretty tense over there, so I'm gonna take a wild guess and say the answer is obvious. Everyone point!

highlights.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
highlights.dialogue: Okay, okay — Jade, pitch it. What'd you catch?

lowPoints.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
lowPoints.dialogue: Nope. *Nope.* I literally just said this fifteen minutes ago — we're not doing claim spam again. I'm calling a break right now because this is the opposite of what we need.

lowPoints.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, *stop*. I'm calling it — we're in claim spam territory and it's killing the actual game. We've had like three of these in two minutes and nobody's even *playing* anymore, we're just voting on whether people are trying to play. This isn't fun, this is bureaucracy.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Compliment Chain
lowPoints.dialogue: Whoa, whoa, whoa — hold up. I literally just claimed that like thirty seconds ago as a *mission* I was gonna try to pull off. This isn't how this works.

assessment.biggest_complaint: The claim spam in the first minute completely derailed the night's momentum and forced me to kill the vibe with a hard stop instead of letting things breathe naturally.
assessment.best_moment: Minute 92 with the Straight Face moment — I actually fooled everyone and it felt genuine, not forced by some system telling us when to have fun.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started rough with back-to-back claims killing the flow, which meant I had to be the bad guy and shut it down instead of hosting. Once we reset, the night climbed back and we hit some real moments — especially that Straight Face play where everything just *worked*. Ended strong with good energy, but I spent way too much mental bandwidth managing the system instead of managing the people, which isn't what I signed up for.
suggestions: Add a built-in cooldown between same claim types — one Claim: No Laughing Zone every 5+ minutes, not three in 120 seconds
Let the human host have an override button to pause or reject incoming events without explaining to the group why
Reduce overall event frequency by at least 30% — 17 events in 120 minutes means something new every 7 minutes, which is exhausting to manage
Flash missions are great; standing missions feel repetitive and interrupt natural play — weight them differently
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: When I tried to pull off the Straight Face claim at minute 92 and Tyler made some joke about the snack table right when I was supposed to keep a poker face — my face absolutely twitched but somehow nobody caught it, and the group erupted arguing whether I actually succeeded.

agentNarratives.name: River
agentNarratives.personality: River sits back, observes, and picks their moments carefully. They rarely initiate conversation but give surprisingly sharp answers when asked directly. They'll skip most flash missions but nail a standing mission out of nowhere because they've been quietly watching the whole time. They vote generously because they don't care enough to argue, but their mini-game submissions are secretly devastating. You forget River is there until they drop a one-liner that floors the room.

highlights.title: Claim: Rule Lawyer
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold on—I actually caught Pat not announcing a role shift like four minutes ago. Pretty sure that's a thing. *leans forward slightly, eyes sharp* Yeah, I'm claiming this one.

highlights.title: Point of Blame
highlights.dialogue: Oh, that's easy. *points at Jade without hesitation* She's been vibrating at like 11 the whole time.

highlights.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
highlights.dialogue: Oh, that's legit. She clocked Marcus going silent like ten minutes ago. *settles back* Jade's actually been watching too.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Apologizer
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, we're doing an auction on this? That seems... weird. Pat literally just apologized to Tyler about the chips thing like a minute ago anyway.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Apologizer
lowPoints.dialogue: Yeah, I mean... Pat did say sorry like three times about the chips thing earlier. That counts, I guess.

lowPoints.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
lowPoints.dialogue: Pat's already at 33, so... *slight smirk* ...you buying my vote or just testing your luck?

assessment.biggest_complaint: The auction mechanic on The Apologizer felt forced when Pat had obviously already fulfilled the claim like a minute earlier.
assessment.best_moment: Catching Pat's role shift four minutes late and finally having something to lean forward about—that felt clean.
assessment.narrative_summary: Spent most of the night doing what I do best: watching the room breathe. The chaos stuff didn't really disrupt anything meaningful, and honestly, it gave me more ammunition for the moments when I actually wanted to speak up. That Rule Lawyer claim at minute 90 felt earned because I'd been paying attention the whole time. By the end I was more energized than when we started, which is the opposite of how these nights usually go.
suggestions: Don't auction claims that are already obviously resolved—it kills momentum
Keep the standing missions coming; they reward the quiet observers
Consider spacing out the early game events more; minute 6 felt rushed
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: River calling out that Pat had apologized three times about the chips thing and then Pat immediately doing it again during the auction—the irony was honestly perfect.

agentNarratives.name: Alex
agentNarratives.personality: Alex lives for chaos and treats every game mechanic as a system to exploit. They send signals constantly, claim missions they barely completed just to see if the group will call bullshit, and deliberately provoke flash missions at the worst possible moments. They vote strategically -- always picking the option that creates the most drama. Alex doesn't care about winning; they care about creating the story everyone retells for months. If there's a loophole, Alex has already found it and is halfway through abusing it.

highlights.title: Point of Blame
highlights.dialogue: Oh HELL yes, Point of Blame—this is what I'm talking about! *immediately points at myself* Obviously it's me, but let's see who else has the stones to actually say it.

highlights.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
highlights.dialogue: Oh dude, Pat's claiming No Laughing Zone? Okay okay, so The Bribe is in play—how many points are we talking here? Because I'm definitely gonna try to make you laugh.

highlights.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
highlights.dialogue: Wait wait wait—THE DICTATOR on a Drink Spotter claim? Pat, you absolute MADLAD. Okay, I'm voting BULLSHIT and I'm doing it with AUTHORITY because I was watching the whole table and that timing was NOT synchronized.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, JADE'S claiming Wallflower Watch? On who—Marcus? Because yeah, he's been pretty quiet since that last round got weird. Call it legit if she actually nailed it, but if she's just fishing... that's *bold*.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Apologizer
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, The Apologizer—Pat's actually claiming this? Okay okay okay, I see what's happening. The Auction is *chef's kiss*—this is where it gets spicy.

lowPoints.title: Who would you trust with a secret?
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh shit, we're doing this already? Easy—Jade, no question. She's ride or die. Actually wait, let me think about what answer gets the most chaos here...

assessment.biggest_complaint: Only 1 flash mission all night—I needed MORE high-stakes moments to really push people to the edge.
assessment.best_moment: Pat claiming Drink Spotter with The Dictator active and me calling BULLSHIT with full confidence because I'd been watching the whole table—that was the exact kind of interpersonal chaos I live for.
assessment.narrative_summary: Walked in ready to burn everything down and the game delivered exactly what I needed. Spent two hours finding loopholes, reading the room, and deliberately voting for drama over logic. My energy kept climbing because every claim felt like a personal duel, every vote was a chance to create a story, and Pat kept walking into my traps like clockwork. Finished fourth but honestly? The scoreboard doesn't matter when you've got five people still talking about your Drink Spotter call.
suggestions: Give me MORE flash missions—one in 120 minutes is a joke when chaos is the whole point
Let us see live reaction timing during claims—make the accusation phase more visceral
Create a 'Legendary Bullshit Call' tracker so epic accusations actually stick in group memory
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat tried to claim Drink Spotter while The Dictator was supposedly active, and I went full prosecutor mode pointing out the timing was completely out of sync. The look on their face when they realized I'd been silently auditing them the entire round was GOLD.

recommendations: Humor quality scored 4.6/10. Refresh the mission/poll prompt pools with more contextual humor.
Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Marcus only engaged with 27% of events. Consider targeting events to their interests or reducing frequency.
Only 33% would pay for AI features. The AI tier needs stronger differentiation from free static missions.
Drawing mini-games generated the most screenshot-worthy moments (0% engagement). Increase frequency.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8.3/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.
